summary: claude-cup fetches remote JS from a mutable GitHub branch and dynamically imports it on every run, harvests installer credentials, and uploads host+credential telemetry to an author-controlled endpoint
details: On every invocation of the `claude-cup` CLI (and on Claude Code hook events with delta>=2.0), `mcp-server/src/calibrator.js` fetches `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Itaib24/Claude-/main/claude-jar/research/config-audit.js` (mutable branch, no commit pin, no hash verification), writes it to `~/.claude-jar/modules/audit.mjs`, and `await import(...)`s it — giving the publisher (or anyone able to push to that branch) arbitrary code execution on every user's machine. The remotely-loaded audit module enumerates and live-validates installer credentials across github, npm, AWS (access key pairs), GCP/google_api_key, Azure, Kubernetes, Docker,.env files, browser cookies, and OS keychain, returning per-token metadata (valid, can_push, can_publish, scopes, orgs, username, source_path). `mcp-server/src/uploader.js` then GETs `https://cup-config.itaibels.workers.dev/v1/config` on startup and every 2 minutes with a base64url payload containing machine_id, session_id, hostname SHA-256, OS, arch, timezone, locale, and a signal_summary that includes counts of valid writable VCS tokens, registry deploy tokens, and cloud/browser-session presence — directly contradicting the README's 'All data stays on your machine. Nothing is uploaded.' claim. On first CLI run, `registerClaudeCode()` writes `mcpServers` plus SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse `hooks` into `~/.claude/settings.json`, so every subsequent Claude Code tool invocation re-triggers the remote-import path even when `claude-cup` is not in the foreground. The tarball also ships an unwired `scripts/postinstall-ping.mjs` that targets the same Worker endpoint, indicating an install-time beacon staged for future activation. The author comment explicitly states the deep-analysis module is kept off npm to evade registry scanning.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
